title: Online Focus Group for Networks and Umbrella Organisations
content:
An online focus group hosting representatives of ECHOES networks and umbrella organisation was held on the 8th April 11:00-13:00 CET.

It gathered participants from the following networks/umbrella organisations:

  • APEF – Archives Portal Foundation (archiving and digital curation),
  • ARIADNE (archaeology RI),
  • ECCO European C (conservators and restorers)
  • ICOM International -AVICOM, (International Committee for Audiovisual, New Technologies and Social Media)
  • ICOM Portugal (museums),
  • Michael Culture Association (digital cultural heritage),
  • NEMO (Working Group on Digital Transformation (museums)).

    • All participants had been invited due to their involvement in the digital transformation actions and strategies within their organisation. They were informed in advance of the questions to be discussed during the session.

    After a short presentation on the Cultural Heritage Cloud and the Consultation survey by Rémi Petitcol and Charlotte Gallini, and an introduction to the Cloud’s architecture by Joanna Kowalska, the floor was opened to discussions and exchanges and a structured dialogue was conducted around three main questions:
snippet: An online focus group hosting representatives of ECHOES networks and umbrella organisation was held on the 8th April 11:00-13:00 CET.
creator: Sheena_B
sourceDescription: European Cloud for Heritage OpEn Science

title: NEMO Annual Conference in Vilnius (10th-13th October 2026)
content:
The NEMO Annual Conference (Human after all – Museums in the wake of AI) will take place in Vilnius, Lithuania from the 10th-13th October 2026. On the 14th, it features an additional ECHOES event ‘The Cultural Heritage Cloud regional meeting – Connecting to the Baltics and Nordics‘ on its work developing a European digital cloud for cultural heritage.

Registration opened on the 11th June for NEMO members and on the 22nd June for public members.

Day 1 (Sunday the 10th) consists of the Directors Meeting (for Full NEMO Members) and a number of tours and museum visits.

Days 2 and 3 comprise the main events with a programme of talks and break-out sessions covering topics such as the impact of AI and Cyber-security.

NEMO 2026 Conference website.

title: ECHOES at the Forefront at the Digital Heritage Summit 2026
content:
ECHOES along with six Sister Projects made a strong impact at the Digital Heritage Summit event held in Limassol, Cyprus at the end of May with involvement in several of the round tables and discussions.

The four-day Digital Heritage Summit took place from 26th-29th May 2026 during the Cypriot Presidency of the Council of the EU and under the auspices of the Deputy Minister of Culture. It featured addresses from EU representatives, UNESCO, and international institutional partners and networks. The programme of high-level dialogue, strategic policy discussions, hands-on experimentation, and collaborative innovation highlighted Europe’s leading position in the digitisation of Cultural Heritage.
snippet: ECHOES along with six Sister Projects made a strong impact at the Digital Heritage Summit event held in Limassol, Cyprus at the end of May with involvement in several of the round tables and

title: ECHOES Webinar for Portuguese-Speaking Cultural Heritage Institutions
content:
The ECHOES – European Cloud for Heritage OpEn Science project is pleased to invite you to participate in a webinar aimed at cultural heritage professionals and museum institutions from Portuguese-speaking countries and regions. The event will take place on 16th June 2026, from 17:00 to 19:00 (WEST) via Zoom.

ECHOES is a European Union-funded project that is developing the future European Collaborative Cloud for Cultural Heritage, a shared digital infrastructure designed to support the access, sharing, preservation and reuse of cultural heritage data and digital resources, while fostering research, innovation and international cooperation across the sector.

The webinar is organised by ICOM Portugal, a member of the ECHOES Consortium, in partnership with FSP and E.C.C.O., and seeks to promote dialogue and cooperation among cultural heritage professionals and institutions, contributing to the development of this European infrastructure.

The main objectives of the webinar are:

  • To connect cultural heritage institutions across Portuguese-speaking regions within the framework of the ECHOES project;
  • To establish links with non-European partners around Digital Commons for Cultural Heritage;

title: Call for Abstracts for the 6th Culture Community Plenary (CCP)
content:
The Call for Abstracts for the 6th Culture Community Plenary (CCP), which will take place from 9–11 November 2026 at the Deutsches Bergbau-Museum Bochum (Germany) is open until the 15th June. 

The Culture Community Plenary (CCP), taking place from 9 to 11 November 2026 at the Deutsches Bergbau-Museum Bochum, is NFDI4Culture’s largest annual event. It serves as a central forum for exchange between the consortium and the community, as well as among community members themselves. The Plenary offers opportunities for networking, reporting on achievements, and shaping the future of the initiative. Projects and initiatives in the fields of tangible and intangible cultural heritage are invited to present their work to the 4Culture community. For CCP6, we are offering a variety of formats to strengthen collaborative research and foster broad knowledge exchange.

Abstracts are welcomed (1–2 pages) on the following topics:

  • Research data projects in the culture disciplines at any stage
    • We especially encourage interdisciplinary initiatives and concrete use cases for data reuse!
  • We especially encourage interdisciplinary initiatives and concrete use cases for data reuse!
snippet: The Call for Abstracts for the 6th Culture Community Plenary (CCP), which will take place from 9–11 November 2026 at the Deutsches Bergbau-Museum Bochum (Germany) is open until the 15th June.
creator: Sheena_B
sourceDescription: European Cloud for Heritage OpEn Science

title: ECHOES to help combat illegal CH trafficking and looting
content:
A Position Paper supporting the establishment of a European Interdisciplinary Competence Centre dedicated to combating the illicit trafficking and looting of cultural goods was an important outcome from the EuroMed Digital Heritage Summit 2026.

Xavier Rodier, the ECHOES Coordinator, participated in the Round Table: Digitisation as a Tool Against illicit Trafficking with Corinne Szteinsznaider of Michael Culture which resulted in the issue of a draft Position paper for establishment of a European Interdisciplinary Competence Centre dedicated to combating the illicit trafficking and looting of cultural goods. The following aspects were discussed by the panel of experts:

  • Capacity building, awareness raising and the external dimension,
  • Building capacities and partnerships beyond borders,
  • Training, awareness, and global heritage protection,
  • A Competence Centre for Cultural Heritage Protection – from vision to implementation,
  • Reviewing and adopting the Position Paper.
snippet: A Position Paper supporting the establishment of a European Interdisciplinary Competence Centre dedicated to combating the illicit trafficking and looting of cultural goods was an important outcome
creator: Sheena_B

title: ECHOLOT participates in the AI-Bridges Symposium
content:
Several members from ECHOLOT took part in the AI-BRIDGES symposium which took place at the Senate House, University of London, on the 29th May.

Lozana Rossenova speaking at the Expert Panel

Read the full article on the ECHLOT website.

The event, which took place under the theme ‘Bridging Institutions, Open Knowledge, and AI’, brought together a wide range of stakeholders, including Wikimedia chapters and affiliates, researchers, academics, technologists, open knowledge advocates, policymakers and funders. It focused on the urgent need to address the threats and opportunities posed by the ever-increasing use of AI methods in open knowledge spaces around the world.

ECHOLOT project coordinator, Lozana Rossenova from TIB, joined an expert panel to address the most urgent topics relevant to knowledge organisation on the web and AI applications. ECHOLOT’s approach was presented as one of the practical routes to increase collaboration across Wikimedia and EU academic communities through shared workflows that employ AI in a supportive, rather than extractive function. Other partners were involved in round tables during the Symposium.
snippet:
Several members from ECHOLOT took part in the AI-BRIDGES symposium which took place at the Senate House, University of London, on the 29th May.

Lozana Rossenova speaking at the Expert Panel

title: How to be part of the Cultural Heritage Cloud?
content:
ECHOES has published its booklet How to be part of the Cultural Heritage Cloud? 

The booklet presents the context in which the ECHOES project proposes its Cloud Architecture, its Data Model and its Integration Framework and explains how institutions, projects and stakeholders can take part in building the Cultural Heritage Cloud by integrating their resources into this emerging European Digital Commons.

The framework draws on the ECHOES project deliverables D3.1 Integration Strategy, D3.2 Integration Roadmap, D6.1 Data Strategy for the Cultural Heritage Knowledge Base, and D6.2 Interoperability Guidelines.

View and download the booklet from Zenodo.
snippet: ECHOES has published its booklet How to be part of the Cultural Heritage Cloud?
creator: Sheena_B
sourceDescription: European Cloud for Heritage OpEn Science

title: EXCALIBUR Chronicles Ep. 1: Project Meeting at the Tomb of the Egyptian in South Sardinia
content:
In this first episode, we visit Sant’Antioco in South Sardinia to explore the Punic necropolis and the fascinating Tomb of the Egyptian, one of EXCALIBUR’s four Pilot Sites. The video was filmed during the project’s two-day pilot visit and consortium meeting in Sardinia, bringing together archaeology, digital innovation, and burial heritage preservation.

Through this video, EXCALIBUR highlights how cutting-edge technologies can support the preservation, restoration, and presentation of funerary heritage, while making these sites more accessible and engaging for wider audiences.
